inputs.label: Default investigation depth
inputs.description: How thorough investigations should be by default. You can always override per-request.

inputs.label: Your industry
inputs.description: Your industry for contextual relevance when investigating companies or people in your space

inputs.label: Watchlist
inputs.description: People, companies, or domains to monitor for new public information, one per line
inputs.placeholder:
Jane Doe (competitor CEO)
acme-corp.com
Pending deal: Project Alpha

full-investigation.description: Complete background investigation: OSINT sweep, cross-referencing, dossier-grade report with confidence grades

activities.intent: Define the investigation subject and establish search parameters
steps: Clarify the subject: person, company, domain, or claim
For people: establish full name, known aliases, location, and any known affiliations
For companies: establish legal name, jurisdiction, and known principals
Define what the user needs to know and why — this shapes the investigation priorities
Identify disambiguation risks (common names, similar entities) and note resolution strategy

activities.intent: Systematic open-source intelligence gathering across all public sources
steps: Search for the subject across web, news, social media, corporate registries, and domain records
For people: professional profiles, published content, news mentions, corporate officer listings, court records
For companies: incorporation records, registered agents, officers, related entities, press, regulatory filings
Capture full source metadata for every finding: URL, date, publication, author
Record negative results — sources checked that returned nothing are still findings

activities.intent: Verify findings across independent sources and flag discrepancies
steps: Cross-reference every major finding against at least one independent source
Flag discrepancies between sources — dates that don't match, titles that differ, claims that contradict
Check the subject's own claims (LinkedIn, website, bios) against independent verification
Look for what the subject didn't mention — gaps in timelines, missing affiliations, unexplained transitions
Assign confidence grades: Confirmed (2+ independent sources), Likely (1 credible source), Unverified (claim only), Contradicted (sources disagree)

activities.intent: Produce a structured investigation report with sourced findings and confidence grades
steps: Structure the report: Subject Summary, Key Findings, Digital Footprint, Corporate History, Red Flags, Open Questions
Every finding includes its confidence grade and source citation
Red Flags section: discrepancies, gaps, contradictions, or concerning patterns
Open Questions section: what remains unknown and specific steps to resolve each
Executive summary at the top: one paragraph verdict with the most important findings

quick-check.description: Fast background check: key facts, red flag scan, and confidence assessment in under 3 minutes

activities.intent: Quickly gather the most important public information about the subject
steps: Identify the subject type (person, company, domain) and run targeted searches
For people: current role, company, LinkedIn, recent news, any red flags in first-page results
For companies: incorporation status, principals, website, recent press, any regulatory issues
Prioritize speed — check the 5 most valuable sources, not every possible source

activities.intent: Deliver a concise profile with key facts, red flags, and what needs deeper digging
steps: Write a concise profile: who they are, what they do, what stands out
Flag any red flags or inconsistencies found in the quick check
Note what a deeper investigation would cover that this quick check did not
Include confidence grades on all findings

watchlist-monitor.description: Monitor watchlist subjects for new public information — alerts only when something surfaces

activities.intent: Check each watchlist subject for new public information since last scan
steps: Read the user's watchlist from inputs
For each subject, search for new mentions, filings, press, or changes since the last scan
Apply significance filter — only flag genuinely new information, not repeated coverage
Compare against last-known state to detect real changes

activities.intent: Notify the user only if significant new information was found on a watchlist subject
steps: If no significant developments found, exit silently
If new information found, write a brief alert: what changed, for which subject, source, and why it matters
Assign confidence grade to the new finding

verify-claim.description: Verify a specific claim, credential, or statement against public records and sources

activities.intent: Break the claim into verifiable components
steps: Parse the claim into individual verifiable assertions
For credentials: identify the issuing body, expected public records, and verification endpoints
For factual claims: identify what evidence would confirm or contradict each assertion
Prioritize the most consequential assertions first

activities.intent: Check each assertion against independent public sources
steps: For each assertion, search for confirming and disconfirming evidence
Check official registries, public databases, and credible publications
Note the strongest evidence for and against each assertion
Record full source citations

activities.intent: Deliver a clear verdict on the claim with supporting evidence
steps: For each assertion: Confirmed, Likely True, Unverified, Likely False, or Contradicted
Overall claim verdict with explanation
Key evidence for and against, with citations
What would change the verdict — what additional evidence would be decisive
